pattern: Convert each person's company list to a set, then for each person check whether their set is a proper subset of any other person's set.
hints: Convert every company list into a Python set / JS Set for O(1) membership checks.
For person i, iterate over all other persons j and test if the set for i is a subset of the set for j.
Collect the indices of persons who are not subsumed by anyone else, already sorted because you iterate in order.

edge_inputs.description: second list is subset of first
all lists are equal size and disjoint
chain: list 0 contains list 1 which contains list 2
one list that is subset of two different lists
subset check must be strict — a list is not a subset of itself (all lists are distinct per constraints)

stress.description: 20 people each with 5-15 companies drawn from a pool of 30
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    pool = ['c' + str(i) for i in range(30)]
    people = []
    seen = set()
    for _ in range(size):
        k = rng.randint(3, 10)
        lst = sorted(rng.sample(pool, k))
        key = tuple(lst)
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            people.append(lst)
    if not people:
        people = [['c0']]
    return [people]
